summary_one_line: Profile device-class FOURCC at offset 0x0c must equal 'nmcl' (cmsSigNamedColorClass) to take the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte device-class signature in the ICC profile header (input bytes at offsets 0x0c-0x0f) must spell the FOURCC 'nmcl' so that cmsGetDeviceClass(hProfile) returns cmsSigNamedColorClass and the equality at cmsxform.c:1025 is satisfied.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Both winner-resolving seeds carry bytes 6e 6d 63 6c ('nmcl') at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, the exact FOURCC for cmsSigNamedColorClass; the W role hits the true side (L1025 W T=2) and only it reaches the named-color body at L1027-1028 (tag [W]).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds carry 6d 6e 74 72 ('mntr', monitor class) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, which is not cmsSigNamedColorClass, so the true branch is never taken (L1025 L T=0 F=48) and the seeds stay pinned to the false side for the full 24h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's integer-CMP interception writes a CMP_MAP bucket keyed by operand-distance (Hamming/prefix) for the cls == cmsSigNamedColorClass comparison; an input whose offset-0x0c bytes are closer to 'nmcl' adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is retained, giving a gradient toward the exact 4-byte match that naive's plain edge feedback lacks.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is an equality test of the profile device class against cmsSigNamedColorClass.
The compared value cls is derived from the profile via cmsGetDeviceClass(hProfile), an input-dependent header field.
Winner seeds contain the FOURCC 'nmcl' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f.
At the offsets where the device-class FOURCC lives, winner bytes 'nmcl' differ from loser bytes 'mntr'.
Loser seeds carry the monitor-class FOURCC 'mntr' at offset 0x0c onward.
The winner takes the true side while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1025  T=2 F=2  T=0 F=48  if (cls == cmsSigNamedColorClass) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile feedback rewards inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching by adding a new CMP_MAP bucket and preserving the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile uses no I2S substitution mutator, so the winner's advantage is the CMP_MAP gradient, not byte splicing.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so a lineage dash-row query is not mandatory. The prompt's MECHANISM CONTEXT states value_profile has 'No `I2SRandReplace`' and that the seed mutation_op rows are 'havoc/token names only', consistent with the CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback being the winner's lever rather than I2S substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating an equality on a 4-byte input field where value_profile fails to outpace naive at reaching the true side, i.e. value_profile shows no CMP_MAP-driven advantage on a multi-byte exact-match comparison.

weakest_evidence_point: The CMP_MAP gradient attribution is inferred from the equality shape plus value_profile's feedback description; the prompt provides no per-bucket CMP_MAP trace proving the gradient was actually walked toward 'nmcl'.
